dsl_rule.translation_notes: N-day breakout strategy translated using Donchian Channel indicators (dc_upper, dc_lower) which represent the highest high and lowest low over a lookback period. Entry long when price breaks above the N-day high (dc_upper), entry short when price breaks below the N-day low (dc_lower). Exit conditions are reversed: exit long on breakout of N-day low, exit short on breakout of N-day high. The specific value of N is not provided by the author (AUTHOR_SILENT), so Donchian channels at their default periods are used as the closest available approximation.

backtest_rule.reason: N-day breakout strategy translated using Donchian Channel indicators (dc_upper, dc_lower) which represent the highest high and lowest low over a lookback period. Entry long when price breaks above the N-day high (dc_upper), entry short when price breaks below the N-day low (dc_lower). Exit conditions are reversed: exit long on breakout of N-day low, exit short on breakout of N-day high. The specific value of N is not provided by the author (AUTHOR_SILENT), so Donchian channels at their default periods are used as the closest available approximation.

source_rule_text: Buy when today's high moves above the high of the past N days; sell when today's low moves below the low of the past N days.
